content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1–4.5.3 WARN — push entitlement present and used for marketing spam: hourly repeating promo notification in `App/NotificationScheduler.swift:12` ("repeats: true", 3600s) plus metadata promising "EVERY HOUR … unlimited notifications" (`fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:3`).
Pierre: Apple's 4.5.4 policy area frowns on push used for unsolicited advertising, and repeating hourly promo pushes ("Hot deal alert!") combined with an App Store description promising unlimited hourly notifications reads as exactly that — this may trigger review questions or rejection. The notifications are not user-initiated alerts (e.g., a price drop the user subscribed to) but blanket marketing. Consider making alerts opt-in per deal/keyword, giving frequency controls, and removing the "EVERY HOUR / unlimited notifications" claim from the metadata.
